what_it_does: This JSON file is an allocation batch that lists six source files from the claude-ds project, each located under the axes/ directory, with project name, relative path, and absolute root directory.
observations: Contains six entries, all from project claude-ds and root /home/tim/company/design/claude-ds
Five entries are .js files and one is motion.css
All entries share an identical object shape: project, path, root
standouts: All entries belong to a single design-system project
The batch mixes one CSS file with otherwise JS axis modules
Paths follow a consistent axes/<axis-name>/<file> layout
conventions: Uses a flat array of uniform {project, path, root} objects
Records paths relative to the project root
Batch is organized under wave-002/alloc/ suggesting a wave-based allocation scheme
concerns: No stated rationale for why these six files are grouped
No version, timestamp, or checksum metadata
notes: A wave-002 allocation batch registering six claude-ds axis modules for interpretive processing.
questions: Why is motion.css included while the other entries are JS modules?
What criterion groups these particular six axis files into batch-020?
Is the absolute root path intended to be portable across environments?
intention_signals: Catalog a coherent slice of a design system's axis modules so an interpretive pipeline can process them together.
contribution: Provides a project-scoped, path-rooted inventory of six axis modules, giving a fused system a concrete batch definition for processing the form, motion, size, space, and symbol axes of claude-ds.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON allocation batch listing six claude-ds design-system axis files under axes/ with project, path, and root fields.
intention_for_embedding: To register a set of related design-system axis modules for wave-002 interpretive processing.
